options: Any foreign molecule that triggers an immune response, including pollen and dust
An organism or agent (bacterium, virus, fungus, prion, or parasite) capable of causing disease in a host
A white blood cell that ingests and destroys bacteria in the bloodstream
A protein produced by the immune system to neutralize toxins
explanation: Pathogens are disease-causing agents; the term encompasses bacteria, viruses, fungi, protists, prions, and parasitic worms that can infect and harm a host.
